# Visual Companion Guide

Use this when a question is easier to answer with a visual than with text.

## When To Use
- UI mockups, wireframes, layouts, or navigation structures.
- Architecture diagrams, flowcharts, or relationship maps.
- Side-by-side design comparisons where appearance matters.

## When Not To Use
- Requirements, scope, or conceptual choices.
- Trade-off lists, technical decisions, or other text-first questions.

## Behavior
- Treat acceptance of the visual companion as permission to use it, not a requirement for every step.
- Decide per question whether text or visuals are better.
- Keep visual screens focused on one decision at a time.

## Practical Guidance
- Use the browser only when the content itself is visual.
- Use the terminal for questions whose answers are words, not visuals.
- If the next step no longer needs visuals, switch back to text-only discussion.
